_meta.note: First real regression pins for the generic literature meta-analysis exemplar (default term 'modafinil'; multi-engine retrieval, de-dup, full-text, embeddings, bibliometrics). The live pipeline makes network calls (multi-engine retrieval); these pins deliberately re-derive every value OFFLINE from the committed deterministic seed corpus data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl (80 synthetic records, reserved 10.5555/ test DOIs, RNG seed 42) via the real source functions src.literature.corpus.Corpus.load + src.analysis.descriptive_stats + src.analysis.temporal_analysis -- the same functions whose outputs manuscript/src/manuscript/variables.py injects into the {{CORPUS_SIZE}}/{{UNIQUE_AUTHORS}}/{{CITATION_MAX}}/{{CITATION_TOTAL}}/{{GINI_COEFFICIENT}}/{{PEAK_YEAR}} tokens in manuscript/03a_results_field_overview.md. No network, no mocks. A live retrieval run replaces the fixture corpus and legitimately shifts these values; when that happens the pins and the manuscript must be updated together per docs/maintenance/regression-testing.md.

field_overview_corpus_size.reason: Number of de-duplicated records in the default offline corpus. Injected as {{CORPUS_SIZE}} in 03a_results_field_overview.md ('N = {{CORPUS_SIZE}} records'). Re-derived as len(Corpus.load(fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl).papers), matching variables.compute_variables corpus_size = len(papers).

field_overview_unique_authors.reason: Distinct author-name count across the corpus, injected as {{UNIQUE_AUTHORS}} in the Descriptive Bibliometrics prose. Re-derived via descriptive_stats(papers)['unique_authors'] -- the exact source variables.py reads for the UNIQUE_AUTHORS token.
field_overview_unique_authors.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.descriptive_stats import descriptive_stats; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(descriptive_stats(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["unique_authors"])'

field_overview_pct_with_doi.reason: Percentage of records carrying a DOI, injected as {{PCT_WITH_DOI}} ('DOI_COUNT of CORPUS_SIZE records (PCT_WITH_DOI%) carry DOIs'). Every synthetic fixture record has a reserved 10.5555/ DOI, so this is exactly 100.0. Re-derived via descriptive_stats(papers)['pct_with_doi'].
field_overview_pct_with_doi.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.descriptive_stats import descriptive_stats; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(descriptive_stats(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["pct_with_doi"])'

bibliometrics_citation_count_max.reason: Maximum citation count in the corpus, injected as {{CITATION_MAX}} ('Citation counts range from zero to CITATION_MAX'). Re-derived via descriptive_stats(papers)['citation_count_max'].
bibliometrics_citation_count_max.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.descriptive_stats import descriptive_stats; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(descriptive_stats(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["citation_count_max"])'

bibliometrics_citation_count_total.reason: Total citations summed across the corpus, injected as {{CITATION_TOTAL}} ('a total of CITATION_TOTAL citations across the corpus'). Re-derived via descriptive_stats(papers)['citation_count_total']; also cross-checked equal to citation_distribution(papers)['total_citations'].
bibliometrics_citation_count_total.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.descriptive_stats import descriptive_stats; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(descriptive_stats(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["citation_count_total"])'

bibliometrics_gini_coefficient.reason: Gini coefficient of citation concentration, injected as {{GINI_COEFFICIENT}} (manuscript renders variables.py's f'{gini:.3f}' = 0.398). Pinned at full float precision to catch sub-display drift; a 1e-9 tolerance covers benign numpy pairwise-difference reordering. Re-derived via citation_distribution(papers)['gini'].
bibliometrics_gini_coefficient.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.descriptive_stats import citation_distribution; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(repr(citation_distribution(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["gini"]))'

field_overview_peak_year.reason: Year with the most publications, injected as {{PEAK_YEAR}} ('peaking in PEAK_YEAR'). Re-derived via compute_temporal_metrics(papers)['peak_year'] -- the source variables.py reads for the PEAK_YEAR token.
field_overview_peak_year.script: .venv/bin/python -c 'from analysis.temporal_analysis import compute_temporal_metrics; from literature.corpus import Corpus; print(compute_temporal_metrics(Corpus.load("data/fixtures/modafinil_corpus.jsonl").papers)["peak_year"])'

field_overview_corpus_size.claim_text: The de-duplicated corpus for modafinil contains $N = {{CORPUS_SIZE}}$ records spanning {{YEAR_START}}--{{YEAR_END}}

field_overview_unique_authors.claim_text: The corpus spans {{UNIQUE_AUTHORS}} unique authors across {{CORPUS_SIZE}} papers

bibliometrics_citation_count_max.claim_text: Citation counts range from zero to {{CITATION_MAX}} (mean {{CITATION_MEAN}}, median {{CITATION_MEDIAN}})

bibliometrics_citation_count_total.claim_text: with a total of {{CITATION_TOTAL}} citations across the corpus

bibliometrics_gini_coefficient.claim_text: The Gini coefficient of citation concentration is {{GINI_COEFFICIENT}} (rendered 0.398)

field_overview_peak_year.claim_text: peaking in {{PEAK_YEAR}} with {{PEAK_YEAR_PUBS}} records that year
